mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns during expansion phases, with a mean forward return difference indistinguishable from zero at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate market repricing of future monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above the 0.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm labor market tightening that supports corporate earnings growth, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to generate positive 60-day SPX returns with a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 20-day SPX direction, with a hit rate below 52% across all tested quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing activity that immediately depresses equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances following two consecutive monthly INDPRO declines.
